name: Joe Henderson
abstract: Joe Henderson (April 24, 1937 – June 30, 2001) was an American jazz tenor saxophonist and occasional flautist. In a career spanning more than four decades, Henderson played with many of the leading American players of his day and recorded for several prominent labels, including Blue Note, Milestone, Contemporary Records and Verve.

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Herbie Hancock Sextet
Carnegie Hall Salutes the Jazz Masters: Verve Records at 50

works.t: No No Nanette: Tea for Two

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Joe Henderson "Double Rainbow" Quintet/ Joshua Redman Quartet/ Dee Dee Bridgewater

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I played this festival and this band was Andrew Hill, Bobby Hutcherson, Ron Carter, Roy Hanyes, Joe Henderson and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Davis
about_quotes.evidence: I heard J.J.’s sound on that in my bedroom... along with, at the time, a young Woody Shaw and Joe Henderson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, December 15, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Stefon Harris
about_quotes.evidence: I remember playing with Joe Henderson and a couple of us in the band
about_quotes.doc_title: Stefon Harris interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 11, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Louis Hayes
about_quotes.evidence: It was Freddie Hubbard and Joe Henderson and Kenny Barron, pianist, and Herbie Lewis was the bassist originally. So Freddie and myself, we, over the years, we played together for all these years and we’re still doing it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louis Hayes interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: This was – it was called the Jazz Communicators. We never recorded. It was Freddie and Joe Henderson, Louis Hayes, Herbie Lewis, and myself, which again was a really great band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Henderson comes along for me, which was a biggie for me... I really was listening to all his recordings... there was a lot of Joe Henderson paid attention to by me.
